title: Smoke-free indoor cooking rule
summary: A hard rule against indoor charcoal, grills, generators, camp stoves, and improvised burners during outages.
content: Cold food and power loss push people toward dangerous indoor cooking. No charcoal, grill, generator, camp stove, or improvised burner belongs in living rooms, stairwells, tents, garages, or closed balconies. Comfortable air is not proof of carbon monoxide safety.
steps: Write the forbidden devices in plain words.
Choose outdoor cooking away from doors, windows, dry grass, and crowds.
Keep children and pets away from flames.
Extinguish and cool equipment before storage.
warnings: Carbon monoxide can kill without obvious smell.
Never run generators indoors or near openings.
Do not bring hot coals inside for warmth.
common_mistakes: Thinking a cracked window makes it safe.
Cooking in a stairwell because it feels outside.
Moving a grill into a closed balcony.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Smoke-free indoor cooking rule with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Smoke-free indoor cooking rule if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Smoke-free indoor cooking rule and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Smoke-free indoor cooking rule or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Smoke-free indoor cooking rule are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Smoke-free indoor cooking rule”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Smoke-free indoor cooking rule as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Smoke-free indoor cooking rule when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Smoke-free indoor cooking rule.
short_term: For the first hours of Smoke-free indoor cooking rule, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Smoke-free indoor cooking rule remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Smoke-free indoor cooking rule fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Smoke-free indoor cooking rule to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
